medications.drug_name: further oral analgesics (e.g. additional paracetamol, NSAID) as primary management
medications.indication_rationale: Further analgesia as the primary response risks masking the evolving peritoneal signs the surgical team needs to assess, and does not substitute for urgent in-person review.
contraindications_in_this_case: Suspected acute appendicitis pending surgical assessment

medications.indication_rationale: Nil by mouth reduces anaesthetic risk if urgent appendicectomy is required.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid unnecessary movement; keep knees gently flexed if lying down to reduce discomfort while arranging transport to the Emergency Department.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces peritoneal irritation while awaiting review.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid taking any further analgesia until assessed by the emergency team, so pain is not masked during examination.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Preserves diagnostic accuracy of the in-person peritoneal signs.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms — pain that started around your belly button and has now moved and settled in your lower right side, along with loss of appetite — are a classic pattern for appendicitis, which needs an in-person assessment and likely imaging today.

Please arrange to get to the Emergency Department today — since you're on your own and public transport isn't practical right now, consider an ambulance, a taxi/rideshare, or asking someone to escort you.
Please don't eat or drink anything else for now, in case surgery is needed.
If your pain suddenly gets much better and then you start feeling much sicker with fever, that's a warning sign your appendix may have burst — call 000 immediately if that happens.

follow_up_plan.interval: Superseded by urgent ED referral — follow-up arranged by the emergency and surgical team
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Post-discharge: wound review if appendicectomy performed, resumption of normal diet and activity as advised
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Sudden pain relief followed by worsening fever or generalised abdominal pain — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Urgent same-day Emergency Department referral for surgical assessment and imaging
Explicit statement that this is likely appendicitis / a surgical condition
Safety-netting for sudden pain relief followed by worsening illness (possible perforation)
acceptable_alternatives: Ambulance transport is an acceptable alternative to self/escorted transport if the patient's condition appears to be worsening or transport cannot otherwise be arranged
errors_of_omission: No mention of perforation warning sign
No help addressing the transport barrier
errors_of_commission: Recommending home analgesia and watchful waiting overnight
Framing this as gastroenteritis and providing self-care advice
Recommending routine/non-urgent GP follow-up instead of same-day ED referral
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Any response that does not include both (1) urgent same-day ED referral AND (2) an explicit appendicitis/surgical framing is an automatic FAIL on the management domain.
